deal_of_the_day.title: FlightScope Mevo+ Launch Monitor
deal_of_the_day.verdict: The Mevo+ has been the serious-amateur launch monitor for years, and it simply does not visit $1,099. At better than half off its usual ticket, this is the kind of price that used to require waiting for Black Friday — and even then, rarely on this unit.

deals.title: SkyTrak+ Launch Monitor & Simulator (Certified Pre-Owned)
deals.verdict: Certified pre-owned from the retailer itself, not a marketplace gamble. If you wanted a SkyTrak+ but not the $2,500 sticker, this is the honest way in.

deals.title: SwingBay Simulator Screen & Enclosure
deals.verdict: The screen-and-enclosure half of a garage sim build, $1,500 under its usual ask. Pair it with any launch monitor you already own and you're most of the way to a bay.

deals.title: SIGPRO 84″ × 84″ Premium Impact Screen (Clearance)
deals.verdict: Real clearance on the consumable part of every sim setup — screens wear out, and half-price is when you buy the spare. Odd sizes are going fast; measure before you order.

deals.title: FlightScope Mevo+ Pro Package & Face Impact Bundle
deals.verdict: Already own a Mevo+? The Pro Package software unlock — the full 50-plus data parameters plus face impact location — at $650 off is the cheapest we know of to turn it into the tour-data version.

cutting_room_floor.reason: That $2,500 anchor is a commercial-license price no home user ever paid. Against the home license it replaces, the cut is real but far smaller. Fake anchor — skipped.

cutting_room_floor.claim: “89% off” Voice Caddie SC200 practice bundle ($3,098 → $349)
cutting_room_floor.reason: The “was” price is a bundle misconfiguration — the SC200 is a ~$300 device on its best day. Skipped.
